Netflix is daring people to cancel with moves like this. My guess is a lot of them will. https://twitter.com/...

What they're really saying is that with plateauing growth and increased competition they need to find creative new ways to nickel and dime customers into paying more money for the same service if they're to keep the insatiable wall street maw well fed

Netflix will ask customers in five Latin America countries to pay a fee if they want to use their account in an additional home, a test it hopes will curb password sharing and generate additional revenue https://www.bloomberg.com/...

not only did Netflix spend a decade clearly stating password sharing was no big deal, they're already limiting the number of concurrent, active streams per account, AND they just imposed a major new price hike.

As @KarlBode notes, Netflix users *already* pay for 1/2/4 concurrent streams. So now you aren't just paying for how many concurrent streams you make, but where they're coming from. Here comes the new cable, same as the old cable. https://twitter.com/...
That's gonna definitely upset the college crowd. Anyone have Netflix subscription on their back-to-school shopping list? https://twitter.com/...

“But today's widespread account sharing between households undermines our long term ability to invest in and improve our service.” Netflix sounds more like Comcast every day. https://www.theverge.com/...

$NFLX customers in Argentina, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras and the Dominican Republic will be asked to pay an extra fee if they use an account for more than two weeks outside of their primary residence, the company said in a blog post Monday. https://about.netflix.com/...

The first piece I've seen that's made explicit what CHIPS truly is: a bailout of Intel under the guise of “American competitiveness” https://www.reuters.com/...

In the ‘skinny’ bill put forward, everything from both sides will be dropped. Ostensibly. Except that Schumer will put some language in it calling for the U.S. to negotiate digital trade agreements, which is what big tech wants.

So Chuck Schumer is going to put a semiconductor subsidy bill on the floor tomorrow, but remove most controversial pieces from it. Except, the rumor is, he is going to be put a little gift to big tech in there, because he can't help himself.🧵

This language won't matter as much as the earlier provisions, but it's still a problem that Schumer - when no one is looking - just continues to help big tech monopolies wherever and however he can. I hope I'm wrong. We'll see tomorrow.

And reminder, @intel and others will sell out their own country and employees unless the government is smart enough to stop it. Capitalism is great, but we have to recognize the flaws. Companies will always sell out to fascist dictatorships if they think they will make some money https://twitter.com/...

UPDATE: Things are moving fast as the CHIPS Act and FABS Act get closer to a vote tomorrow. Major fabless players including AMD are now saying they'll support the legislation despite the lack of a design credit. Story updated - https://www.reuters.com/...
Yea this stripped down FABS act is bad. It needs to also support designers too or else we are not fixing the “China chip” problem. https://twitter.com/...

Major U.S. chip firms are coming around to support a package of chip industry subsidies despite earlier concerns that legislation awaiting a vote in the Senate disproportionately benefits manufacturers like Intel https://www.reuters.com/... https://twitter.com/...

So on the semiconductor bill, originally the Senate had a nasty provision to help big tech circumvent anti-monopoly rules. But the House had a bunch of anti-big tech provisions in their version. https://prospect.org/... https://twitter.com/...

“You have Intel that might get $20 billion with CHIPS Act plus $5 billion or $10 billion under the FABS Act. So $30 billion goes to your direct competitor, and you don't get a penny?” >> U.S. chip industry split over CHIPS act benefits to Intel https://www.reuters.com/...

Umm, No. Fabless companies shouldn't get subsidies just because fab companies do. The business models are fundamentally different. Keep the FABS Act clean and address this separately if Congress wants. Get CHIPS and FABS passed. ASAP https://www.reuters.com/...
Looks like the CHIPS bill to finance semiconductor fabs in the U.S. will also be financing semiconductor fabs in China. Everyone wins! https://twitter.com/...

“A rift is emerging w/in the #chips industry..."#Intel may get $20B w/CHIPS Act + $10B under FABS Act. So $30B goes to your direct competitor & you don't get a penny? That'll cause problems in the mkt...as this will benefit just a few cos" @scottlincicome https://www.reuters.com/...

New w/ @KYWeise: Amazon's new CEO Andy Jassy is breaking from Jeff Bezos, including with a hands-on approach to Washington. — He quietly met w/ Ron Klain — Opposed tech antitrust bill — Lobbied the Commerce secretary on rules Amazon opposes in Europe https://www.nytimes.com/...

what's fun about this profile of andy jassy's political approach in 2022 is that you could replace his name/amazon with sundar/google and set the story a few years ago, and some of it would still ring true https://www.nytimes.com/...

“And while he has reiterated the company's opposition to unions, he has also struck a more conciliatory tone with Amazon's 1.6 million employees.” lol you can just write anything these days https://www.nytimes.com/...

🚨MAJOR INVESTIGATION: uncovering #GeckoSpy. An espionage operation using #Pegasus spyware against #Thailand's pro-democracy movement. THREAD on our findings 1/ Our @citizenlab collaborators: @iLawFX & @DigitalReachSEA w/validation by @AmnestyTech https://citizenlab.ca/... https://twitter.com/...

4/ The #Pegasus hacking came in waves. Some pauses were probably dictated by things outside #Thailand. Like the #PegasusProject publication, our disclosure of #ForcedEntry & @Apple's patch... and those notifications. Other sequences of infection have a contextual explanation... https://twitter.com/...

4/ Once notification recipients got in touch, forensic artifacts were consensually collected & analyzed. The investigation then expanded to associates & other likely #Pegasus targets. I cannot overstate the importance of @apple's notifications in focusing the initial process.

3/ The #GeckoSpy investigation began in Nov 2021... when @Apple notified users likely targeted w/#NSOGroup's FORCEDENTRY exploit. Multiple activists in #Thailand received them. Some got in touch with us @citizenlab & our collaborators including @iLawFX & @DigitalReachSEA https://twitter.com/...

Pegasus is a monitoring system that can capture audio, pictures, texts, contacts, emails and all messages on a phone, including those that are strongly encrypted... The most effective exploits do not need the phone's owner to click on anything to be installed silently. https://twitter.com/...

One year after the first major #Pegasus revelations, another grave misuse uncovered in #Thailand And again, this was the work of investigative journalists and scientists and not the willingness of security apparatus or NSO to come clean. Mistrust is growing by the day... https://twitter.com/...
GeckoSpy: new @citizenlab report details an extensive #Pegasus spyware campaign targeting Thai pro-democracy protesters, and activists calling for reforms to the monarchy. https://citizenlab.ca/...

More than 30 Thai activists and supporters have been hacked with Pegasus spyware, civil groups said late Sunday, in the first countrywide campaign brought to light because Apple warned targeted iPhone users. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

Targets included most of the major figures in the 2020 protests, the prominent Thammasat University academic Prajak Kongkirati, and the leader of the well-known pro-constitutional reform organization iLaw. Full @citizenlab report here: https://citizenlab.ca/.... https://twitter.com/...

NEW REPORT: Discovery of an extensive espionage campaign in Thailand 🇹🇭 involving the abuse of NSO Group's #Pegasus spyware In collaboration with @iLawFX and @DigitalReachSEA https://citizenlab.ca/...

NSO has always said its spyware is exclusively used to combat terrorism and crime. Left unsaid is that every country has its own definition of “terrorist.” In too many cases, for too many NSO clients, journalists, human rights activists, even just vocal citizens all qualify. https://twitter.com/...

Vendors like NSO claim to sell their products to combat “terrorism and crime” but again and again the “crime” is being pro-democracy or speaking out. No plausible deniability for any NSO employees any more. They are making a choice by working there. https://twitter.com/...

More than 30 Thai activists and supporters have been hacked with NSO Group's potent Pegasus spyware, civil groups said late Sunday, in the first countrywide campaign brought to light because Apple warned targeted iPhone users. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
New: NSO Group's Pegasus spyware was used against dozens of Thai activists and supporters, investigators say, including a prominent actress and a rapper. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

NEW: @citiznenlab found NSO Group's Pegasus spyware on Thai civil society phones. One of the victims - Panusaya Sithijirawattanakul - a young woman jailed for wearing a crop top that offended Thai king Sounds like the type of “terrorist” that NSO claims its spyware is made for https://twitter.com/...
